name: autowaka
description: Rule-driven Wakatime Projects Extension for VSCode

autowaka.rules.markdownDescription: Array of rules that determine when and how `.wakatime-project` files are auto-created. Rules are evaluated by proximity; the rule targeting a directory **closest** to the workspace folder wins, regardless of array order.

pathPattern.description: Regex matched against the workspace folder's absolute path. Use .* to match all folders.

requireGitRepo.description: Only trigger this rule if a .git/ directory exists in the folder.

scanNestedGitRepos.markdownDescription: If true, AutoWaka will recursively search for nested Git repositories within any workspace folder that matches this rule's pathPattern, and apply this rule to them.

projectName.markdownDescription: The name of the WakaTime project. You can use placeholders like `{folder}`, `{parent}`, and `{parentN}` to dynamically generate names infinitely upwards. E.g. `Work: {parent3}/{folder} (Client)`.

syncGitExcludes.markdownDescription: If true, synchronizes patterns from `.gitignore` and `.git/info/exclude` into WakaTime's local `.wakatime` file.

autowaka.existingFileBehavior.markdownDescription:
What to do when a `.wakatime-project` file already exists in a matched folder.
- **skip**: Do nothing.
- **prompt**: Ask whether to update.
- **overwrite**: Always overwrite with the rule-generated name.

autowaka.showStatusBar.description: Show the current WakaTime project name in the status bar.

scripts.compile: pnpm run check-types && pnpm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: pnpm run check-types && pnpm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: pnpm run compile-tests && pnpm run compile && pnpm run lint
